title: Tactics: Attraction and Trapping
summary: Lure enemy pieces to squares where they can be trapped or attacked.
description: Attraction (or trapping) is a tactical theme where you lure an enemy piece to a specific square - usually one where it can be attacked, trapped, or where it will cause problems. This is often combined with other tactical themes like pins or discovered attacks.
objectives: Recognize when you can lure pieces to bad squares
Understand how attraction leads to checkmate
Master combining attraction with other themes
plans: Look for squares where an enemy piece would be vulnerable
Create threats that force the piece to that square
Often involves sacrificing to attract the piece
commonMistakes: Don't attract a piece to a square where it can escape
Calculate the full sequence - the attraction should be forcing
Make sure you can follow up after the attraction
completionSummary: You've learned to use attraction tactics to trap enemy pieces.

steps.title: Control the center
steps.hint: Push the king pawn two squares.

steps.title: Recapture
steps.hint: Capture on d4 with the c-pawn.

steps.title: Black develops
steps.instruction: Black moves the bishop to b6.

steps.title: Push e5
steps.instruction: Play e5! This attacks the knight.

steps.title: Knight to g4
steps.instruction: Black moves the knight to g4, attacking h2.
steps.coaching: The knight attacks h2. But we've set up a trap...

steps.title: The attraction!
steps.instruction: Play d5! This lures the knight to a square where it will be trapped!
steps.coaching: The pawn on d5 attacks the knight on g4. Black might take it, but then we can trap the knight!

steps.title: Knight captures
steps.coaching: Now the knight is on e5 - let's trap it!

steps.title: Trap the knight!
steps.instruction: Play Nf4! This attacks the knight on e5 and threatens to trap it!
steps.coaching: The knight attacks e5. Now the black knight has few squares!

steps.title: Knight moves
steps.coaching: The knight moves, but we've gained a pawn and the initiative!
